mnemonics.title: SSH Port Number
mnemonics.content: Remember: 22 = 'Two Too' - SSH is 'too' secure for port 21 (FTP)

mnemonics.title: Port Forwarding Types
mnemonics.content: LAD: Local (-L) Always Destination, Remote (-R) Reverse direction, Dynamic (-D) like a DAM (SOCKS proxy)

mnemonics.title: SSH Authentication Methods
mnemonics.content: PKA: Password, Keyboard-interactive, Public key Authentication

acronyms.usage: Cryptographic network protocol for secure communication
Command-line utility for secure file transfer
FTP-like interface over SSH with more features
Current secure version with improved encryption

visualTips.visualization: Draw client on left (laptop), secure tunnel in middle, server on right (server icon). Show arrows: connect request →, encrypted data ↔, authentication ✓
Local: Local App → Local Port → SSH Tunnel → Remote Port → Remote Service. Remote: Remote Port → SSH Tunnel → Local Port → Local App. Dynamic: App → Local SOCKS → SSH Tunnel → Any Remote

analogies.analogy: Like a military convoy tunnel - safe passage through enemy territory (unsecured network)
Like having a secure ID badge (private key) that matches a locked door's keyhole (public key on server)
Like a valet key - you give your car (private key) once to the agent, and it handles unlocking multiple doors (connections) for you
